title: Google's Gemini Spark Is a 24/7 AI Agent That Works While You Sleep
subtitle: Running on dedicated cloud VMs and powered by Gemini 3.5, Spark takes on long-horizon tasks autonomously — beta hits Google AI Ultra subscribers next week
excerpt: Google announced Gemini Spark at I/O 2026: a personal AI agent that runs around the clock on Google Cloud, handles your inbox, calendar, and web tasks, and will soon operate inside Chrome itself.
seo_title: Google Gemini Spark: 24/7 Personal AI Agent Announced at I/O 2026
seo_description: Gemini Spark is Google's new always-on personal AI agent. Runs on dedicated VMs, handles tasks in Chrome, integrates with Workspace, and rolls out to AI Ultra subscribers next week.
body:
Google's Answer to the Personal Agent Race

The most consumer-facing announcement from Google I/O 2026 was Gemini Spark, a personal AI agent that does not wait for you to open an app. Spark runs continuously on dedicated virtual machines on Google Cloud, meaning it operates 24/7 — with or without your laptop or phone being on.

"Spark is your personal AI agent in the Gemini app that helps you navigate your digital life, taking action on your behalf and under your direction," CEO Sundar Pichai said on stage.

How It Works

Spark is powered by Gemini 3.5 Flash and Google's Antigravity agent harness — the same infrastructure the company uses internally for its own AI developer tooling. Key capabilities at launch:

  • Long-horizon background tasks — Spark can be assigned multi-step jobs (research a topic, draft a doc, book a meeting) and execute them while you go about your day.
  • Deep Workspace integration — It connects to your Gmail, Calendar, Docs, Drive, and Tasks out of the box, with permission-gated actions.
  • Third-party tools via MCP — In the coming weeks, Spark will integrate with external services through the Model Context Protocol.
  • Accessible anywhere — Via the Gemini app today, and soon through email and chat. On Android, a new UI layer called Android Halo will surface live task progress from Spark later this year.
  • Agentic browser — Later this summer, Spark will operate directly inside Chrome, acting as your AI agent across the open web.

The Guardrails

Google was deliberate about framing the consent model. Spark is designed to ask before performing high-stakes actions — sending emails, adding calendar events, making purchases. Users control which capabilities are enabled. The agent runs in Google's cloud infrastructure, which Google positioned as a security advantage over client-side alternatives.

Rollout Timeline

  • This week — Trusted tester rollout begins.
  • Next week — Beta available to Google AI Ultra subscribers in the U.S. ($200/month plan).
  • Summer 2026 — Chrome integration arrives; Android Halo later in the year.
  • Business customers — Gemini Spark in Workspace is coming soon in preview for enterprise.

The Competitive Picture

Spark arrives as every major AI lab is racing toward the persistent agent model. Anthropic has Claude with computer use; OpenAI has Operator; Microsoft has Copilot Actions. Google's differentiation is infrastructure depth — Spark runs on the same TPU-backed cloud Google uses to serve its own products at scale, and has native access to the largest suite of consumer data (Search, Gmail, Maps, YouTube) of any competitor.

Whether users will trust a 24/7 background agent with that level of access is a different question. Google's bet is that convenience wins if the guardrails hold.
